diagrams.title: Fibonacci Recursion Tree Showing Overlapping Subproblems
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the recursion tree for F(5), highlighting how F(3) is computed twice, F(2) is computed three times, demonstrating overlapping subproblems.
elements: Highlighted duplicate nodes: F(3) appears twice, F(2) appears three times
Arrow labels showing '+' operation combining subproblems
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate why naive recursion is inefficient and how DP eliminates redundant calculations by memoization.

diagrams.title: Pascal's Triangle for Binomial Coefficients
diagrams.description: A triangular arrangement of numbers where row n contains values C(n,0), C(n,1), ..., C(n,n). Each element is sum of two elements above it.
elements: Arrows showing element = sum of two elements above-left and above-right
diagrams.purpose: Visual representation of computing binomial coefficients using dynamic programming with tabulation.

diagrams.title: Catalan Numbers as Binary Search Trees
diagrams.description: Showing the 5 different BSTs possible with 3 distinct keys (1,2,3), which corresponds to C₃=5.
elements: Root 1: right child 2, right child of 2 is 3
Root 1: right child 2, left child of 2 is 3
Root 3: left child 1, left child of 1 is 2
Root 3: left child 2, left child of 2 is 1
Labels showing each structure counts as 1
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate real-world application of Catalan numbers in counting binary search tree structures.
